descriptionInUI: Combat SAR runtime built on a single survivor ledger (414th rework). When a pilot ejects, a downed pilot is spawned with red smoke and tracked in one ledger; a friendly helo - player- or AI-crewed - that lands low and slow next to the survivor boards them, and delivering to any friendly field/FARP spares that aviator in the campaign (the airframe is still lost). The enemy may race a snatch party to seize the survivor first - if they hold on the pilot they are taken as a recoverable POW. With the 'Automatic Combat SAR' campaign setting on, an AI rescue helo is dispatched automatically and AI ejections count too, so AI rescues AI (credited) just like a player rescue. C-130 flights tasked Combat SAR fly the 'King' overhead orbit, light an air-tracking TACAN beacon, and carry a 'LARS' F10 button listing every active survivor (position, bearing/range from the King). Inert unless at least one Combat SAR flight is in the mission (the generator emits dcsRetribution.CombatSAR only then).

specificOptions.nameInUI: Pickup distance: how close a helo must be to board the survivor (m)
Pickup ceiling: max height AGL the helo may be to board (m)
Pickup speed: max ground speed the helo may have to board (km/h)
Delivery distance: how close to a friendly field counts as rescued (m)
AI auto-rescue: seconds before an AI helo launches for a downed pilot
Enemy capture race: the enemy may try to seize a downed pilot before rescue
Capture race: chance (%) an ejected pilot draws an enemy snatch party
Capture race: how far from the survivor the snatch party appears (m)
Capture race: how close the party must reach the survivor to seize them (m)
Capture race: seconds the party must hold on the survivor to capture them
Capture race: number of infantry in the enemy snatch party
